accepted_class_exceptions.exception_reason: Reusable async market-data client keeps rate-limit, retry, pagination, session, and fixture behavior in one public import contract.
accepted_class_exceptions.owner_note: Split only with dedicated CoinGecko client parity tests; this pass intentionally avoids provider behavior churn.
accepted_class_exceptions.revisit_condition: Revisit when adding a new CoinGecko endpoint family or changing async session/retry ownership.

accepted_class_exceptions.exception_reason: Field-rich canonical identity value object; splitting fields from serialization would add indirection without reducing behavior risk.
accepted_class_exceptions.owner_note: Keep schema-adjacent identity fields together while canonical resolver contracts settle.
accepted_class_exceptions.revisit_condition: Revisit when schema v2 splits asset identity, venue symbols, and diagnostics into separate contracts.

accepted_class_exceptions.exception_reason: Fixture/live market enrichment adapter is below file-size gates and tightly coupled to request budgeting and no-live defaults.
accepted_class_exceptions.owner_note: Provider activation safety is more important than shaving this adapter below 75 lines.
accepted_class_exceptions.revisit_condition: Revisit when watchlist market enrichment gains another provider implementation.

accepted_class_exceptions.exception_reason: Signed/public announcement compatibility core preserves old imports and WebSocket/fixture behavior.
accepted_class_exceptions.owner_note: Do not split further without signed-listener fixture parity and secret-redaction tests.
accepted_class_exceptions.revisit_condition: Revisit when Binance public rehearsal becomes a first-class activated provider path.

accepted_class_exceptions.exception_reason: Small HTTP announcement adapter is only slightly over the advisory class limit and already package-scoped.
accepted_class_exceptions.owner_note: Avoid touching Bybit request/normalization behavior outside provider-activation work.
accepted_class_exceptions.revisit_condition: Revisit when adding another Bybit announcement endpoint or response shape.

accepted_class_exceptions.exception_reason: Compatibility class still owns request hygiene, token redaction, quota/ledger telemetry, parser normalization, and fixture/live no-call behavior.
accepted_class_exceptions.owner_note: The package already has client/parser/request_ledger homes; moving the remaining class body should be its own provider parity pass.
accepted_class_exceptions.revisit_condition: Revisit when CryptoPanic live activation or request-ledger semantics change.

accepted_class_exceptions.exception_reason: No-key public provider is fail-soft and only slightly over the advisory limit.
accepted_class_exceptions.owner_note: Keep current timeout/429 behavior stable; public-provider noise is expected.
accepted_class_exceptions.revisit_condition: Revisit when adding a second GDELT mode or durable request ledger.

accepted_class_exceptions.exception_reason: Small no-key prediction-market provider keeps fixture/live parsing in one stable adapter.
accepted_class_exceptions.owner_note: Split only when another prediction-market provider is added.
accepted_class_exceptions.revisit_condition: Revisit when Polymarket Gamma support grows beyond the current parser.

accepted_class_exceptions.exception_reason: RSS/Atom adapter is tightly coupled to per-feed fail-soft behavior and source normalization.
accepted_class_exceptions.owner_note: Keep feed failure semantics stable unless adding a reusable RSS client layer.
accepted_class_exceptions.revisit_condition: Revisit when project-blog sources get persistent request ledgers or richer feed classes.

accepted_class_exceptions.exception_reason: OpenAI relationship provider keeps request assembly, timeout/error handling, and structured parsing together behind explicit opt-in gates.
accepted_class_exceptions.owner_note: Do not alter LLM provider behavior during a refactor-only pass.
accepted_class_exceptions.revisit_condition: Revisit when adding a second live LLM backend or shared OpenAI transport abstraction.

accepted_class_exceptions.exception_reason: Small extraction provider is barely over the advisory threshold and shares safety semantics with the relationship provider.
accepted_class_exceptions.owner_note: Keep quote-validation and no-live defaults stable.
accepted_class_exceptions.revisit_condition: Revisit with a broader OpenAI provider transport split.

accepted_class_exceptions.exception_reason: SQLite migration ownership is intentionally centralized to avoid untested schema drift.
accepted_class_exceptions.owner_note: DB schema behavior must not change in this cleanup pass.
accepted_class_exceptions.revisit_condition: Revisit only with explicit migration tests and backup/restore verification.

accepted_class_exceptions.exception_reason: Signal persistence methods share schema assumptions, row serialization, and outcome lookup behavior.
accepted_class_exceptions.owner_note: Avoid splitting storage write paths without SQLite roundtrip parity tests.
accepted_class_exceptions.revisit_condition: Revisit when storage schema v2 or a repository layer is introduced.

accepted_class_exceptions.exception_reason: Watchlist persistence methods are stable DB helpers and only slightly exceed the advisory limit.
accepted_class_exceptions.owner_note: No DB schema or paper/watchlist behavior changes in this refactor pass.
accepted_class_exceptions.revisit_condition: Revisit when watchlist storage grows new tables or migrations.

dead_duplicate_code_removal_note: No obviously dead duplicate top-level Event Alpha code was removed in this pass; old module paths remain available until shim reports and import tests prove retirement is safe.

deprecation_plan.policy: Old top-level Event Alpha imports remain active compatibility shims; new work imports new package paths.
Old imports may warn in development mode only after old/new import tests, Make targets, and operator docs prove compatibility.
Old imports can be removed only through an explicit compatibility-breaking migration with full verification and release notes.

legacy_artifact_doctor_core_note: Behavior-compatible doctor implementation preserved while public artifact_doctor.py is the small orchestrator.

line_gates.risk: Burning down the transitional compatibility core can change CLI defaults, Make target behavior, provider guardrails, or research-only side-effect gates if moved without command snapshots.

line_gates.next_migration_module: tests/rsi, tests/cli, and tests/event_alpha for any remaining umbrella-only cases
line_gates.risk: Over-aggressive removal could break the standalone compatibility runner expected by Make and AGENTS.md.

line_gates.next_migration_module: crypto_rsi_scanner/event_alpha/doctor/checks/safety.py, namespace.py, stale_artifacts.py, and focused legacy counter plugins
line_gates.risk: Doctor extraction can silently change strict/WARN semantics, report counter names, or stale namespace handling if compatibility tests do not pin output.
